content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Charles o. Lamoree 
County Counsel 
County of Solano 
County Courthouse 
Fairfield, CA 
94533 
Dear Mr. Lamoree: 
April 22, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Follow-Up 
Advice to Letter No. A-86-034i 
Our File No. A-86-12~ 
You have written requesting follow-up advice, as follows: 
An integral characteristic of the Solano County 
fair is its recognition and commemoration of 
local community groups and institutions. 
To 
this end, the fair board designates each day of 
the fair in honor of two or three targeted 
County groups or entities. 
Designated days 
typically include Fairfield Day, Travis Air 
Force Base Day, Senior citizens Day, Suisun 
Day, California Maritime Academy Day, Kids Day, 
Rio Vista Day, Agriculture Day, Italian Day, 
Marine World/Africa USA Day, Navy Day, Marine 
Day, Benicia Day, Industry Day, Coast Guard 
Day, Organized Labor Day, Dixon Day and 
Philippine Day, among others. 
The fair board extends courtesy invitations to 
the fair and ceremonial luncheon to a broad 
group of people within each of these categories 
for the day their group is highlighted. 
The 
luncheon constitutes an official reception for 
representatives of each day's targeted 
community groups and others who are being 
particularly recognized. 
Due to their broad duties to the community as a 
whole and their ultimate responsibility for 
managing the fair, the County supervisors are 
inevitably invited and, indeed, expected to 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 .. Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Charles o. Lamoree 
April 22, 1986 
Page 2 
attend the fair on numerous occasions. 
They 
attend and participate in these receptions, 
with other community leaders, officials and 
representatives, in their official capacity. 
For this reason, it would appear to me that 
lunch passes for all days on which a Supervisor 
attends the county fair are properly deemed 
compensation from the County. 
CONCLUSION 
The analysis in your last paragraph is correct so long as 
the passes are actually used by the Supervisors (and a family 
member) for their own meal and not passed on as a gift to 
others. 
REL:sm' 
Sincerely,

OFFICE OF 
COUNTY COUNSEL 
COUNTY COURT HOUSE 
FAIRFIELD. CALIFORNIA 94533 
(707) 429-6491 
CHARLES O. LAMOREE 
COUNTY COUNSEL 
March 28, 1986 
Mr. Robert Leidigh 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Legal Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
THOMAS H. GORDINIER 
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
HARRY B. WYETH 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
JOHN P. DEAN 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
J. PAUL COAN 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
DWIGHT V. NELSEN 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
VICKI L. SIEBER 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
MARC! R. COGUANESE 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
Thank you for your response, dated February 19, 1986, to my request 
for formal written advice (FPPC File No. A-86-034). 
In answer to my 
query whether the fair package sent to County Supervisors is report-
able as a gift on their Statements of Economic Interest, you con-
cluded that two season admission books, one preferred parking book, 
and two lunch tickets should be deemed compensation while the 
remainder of the package should be reportable as a gift under 
Government Code Section 87207(a). 
I would request that you re-
evaluate, based on the information hereinbelow provided, your 
conclusion that only two of the lunch passes represent compensation 
from the County to the Supervisors. 
An integral characteristic of the Solano County fair is its recog-
nition and commemoration of 
local community groups and institutions. 
To this end, the fair board designates each day of the fair in honor 
of two or three targeted County groups or entities. 
Designated days 
typically include Fairfield Day, Travis Air Force Base Day, Senior 
Citizens Day, Suisun Day, California Maritime Academy Day, Kids Day, 
Rio Vista Day, Agriculture Day, Italian Day, Marine World/Africa USA 
Day, Navy Day, Marine Day, Benicia Day, Industry Day, Coast Guard 
Day, Organized Labor Day, Dixon Day and Philippine Day, among others. 
The 
fai 
board ext nds 
ourtesy in itations to 
the 
fair 
a d 
c 
emonial I ncheon to a b oad g oup of pe pI 
w th 
ea h of 
se 
atego ies 
fo 
t e day thei 
gro p is highligh ed. 
he lun heon 
constitutes an official reception for representatives of each day's 
targeted community groups and others who are being particularly 
recognized. 


Robert Leidigh 
March 28, 1986 
Page 2 
Due to their broad duties to the community as a whole and their 
ultimate responsibility for managing the fair, the County Supervisors 
are inevitably invited and, indeed, expected to attend the fair on 
numerous occasions. 
They attend and participate in these receptions, 
with other community leaders, officials and representatives, in their 
official capacity. 
For this reason, it would appear to me that lunch 
passes for all days on which a Supervisor attends the County fair are 
properly deemed compensation from the County. 
Thank you very much for your attention in this matter. 
I am avail-
able at (707) 429-6491 to respond to any questions you may have. 
COL:SKJ:ann 
Very truly yours, 
CHARLES O. LAMOREE 
County Counsel 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Charles O. Lamoree 
Solano County Counsel 
County Court House 
Fairfield, CA 
94533 
Dear Mr. Lamoree: 
April 18, 1986 
Re: 
86-125 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commissioff~ I If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex'legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
REL:plh 
Very truly yours,

member) for their own meal and not passed on as a gift to 
others.
